Glowing Worm Bile  
 

No Trade
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Glowing Worm Bile
No Trade
WT: 1.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL
Race: ALL

Item Type:Misc
Light:Large Lantern
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Agamenoar
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-19 01:45:03
Page Updated:Sat Jun 8th, 2013

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 35
[Drops | Quests | Recipes | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Great Divide
NPC Name
a young shardwurm
an elder shardwurm

Quests

This item is used in quests.
Expansion List - Premium only.

Thurgadin
Quest Name
Coldain Shawl #5: Silk Coldain Prayer Shawl
Used in 1 recipe.
Recipe list - Premium only.

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Great Divide


Uploaded September 3rd, 2017 by Nniki
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
afk spot
QuoteReply
# Sep 08 2024 at 8:28 PM Rating: Good
jebler
Sage
134 posts
/way -4168, -180, -175

after running around tracking every wurm in the zone for 4 or 5 spawns i decided to take a break right near the middle of the main cave with the river flowing thru it. were it expands out a little and has a static spawn thats always an elder. figured i might get a lucky drop wile i had lunch. came back and had 3 new biles. turns out a lot of the roaming ones in the caves pass by here. i wish a knew about this spot years ago.

glowing biles

4 posts
took about 180-190 young and elder shardwurm kills in Great Divide to get 5 of these glowing biles

Glowing Worm Bile drops
QuoteReply
# Jul 07 2017 at 12:46 AM Rating: Decent
Kdor
View User Forum
Scholar
64 posts
Took about 5 hours to get 10 drops.

All from elders and youngs... both inside and outside of tunnels.

Drops at all times of day, not just at night.

At night, I didn't notice any "glowing" mobs, even when they dropped the bile.

not uncommon
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2015 at 9:04 AM Rating: Excellent
tatankaseventh
Guru
783 posts
I just farmed up 10 of these for two alts doing the shawl quests. Took a total of 222 young and elder wurm kills, so that makes the drop rate just under 5%. So yeah, fairly uncommon.

Tat
____________________________
Tatanka Wolfdancer, 115 Wood Elf Druid, 9 x 300+ Master Artisan, 7 maxed trophies (dang research! :)
Michone, 115 Troll Shadowknight
Anaceup Mysleeves, 115 Erudite Mage, 2 x 300 Master Artisan
Snookims Whinslow, 112 Erudite Enchanter, 2 x 300 Master Artisan

▲ Page top
I did get drops in the wurm cave.
QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2013 at 6:09 PM Rating: Decent
Vengaia
Scholar
17 posts
First the wurm cave is on the map in the middle of the great divide - the river running north/south runs through the wurm caves so if you follow the river, you will find these caves. I can confirm that only elder and young worms drop the bile. I found 4 out of 5 of mine from elder wurms in the caves. I am 91 so kills were fast and easy, I just killed everything in the caves starting at the north and working south. Once the caves were cleared I headed out of the south end of the caves and killed 2-4 young / elder wurms roaming in the open field south of the caves. Then rinse and repeat. I was able to sit in a passageway in the cave afk for awhile and my pet killed any wurms passing through - this is how I got my last bile.
▲ Page top
Worm bile drops in cave
QuoteReply
# Sep 17 2012 at 1:53 PM Rating: Decent
Kwatroki
3 posts
I can confirm that I looted a glowing worm bile off an elder shardwurm killed in the caves last night. So the drop is by mob, not location, but you're still better off hunting outside where there are fewer non-bilophoric wurms.
▲ Page top
shards
QuoteReply
# May 17 2012 at 10:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1711930557566
13 posts
confirmed, I killed hundreds of the ancients, crystaline and guardians wurms without a single drop then refered to here to see what drops them and after about an hour of killing elders and young i got them all. save yourself the hassle.
▲ Page top
Bile
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2012 at 10:50 PM Rating: Decent
Ayekinheelya
108 posts
I got three of these on the Dru within a very short time from Elders on the North side of the caves. I didn't kill any Young, and none seemed to drop from the Ancients or Guardians, and like others have said none on the inside of the caves, only outside. I found that to be true, even the ones I pulled to the outside from the inside would have them, though. I had no idea what they were for until I just now looked it up. I'd be interested to see if things turned around for Starwhisp should he give it a go on the Northside!



Edited, Jan 19th 2012 10:55pm by Ayekinheelya
____________________________
72nd Cleric of the Nameless


▲ Page top
some sort of trigger
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2011 at 11:48 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1694996217190
Sage
156 posts
i killed these for hours. no drops in caves. focused primarily on those south of caves, per posts here. no drops.

got my 75 silk threads in velketor's labyrinth and then returned. turned down my gamma, the game hours was after midnight.

i seriously got drops within 5 minutes. i could definitely tell which ones had the bile as they emit a light source.

question is: why the sudden change from NO drops to 2/3 having the item?

there were 3 things:
a) i lowered my gamma
b) i had 75+ threads in my inventory
c) the hour was after midnight and it was DARK in the zone

i readily discard (a) because it is silly. the gamma has nothing to do with it. it DOES make it easier to see which mobs have the item, but i was killing every mob in sight- there are only 6 or so up at any given time, south of the caves, anyway.

(b) is a possibility but i am inclined to go with (c) - reason being:

every other time i was in the zone, it happened to be daylight hours- and i got NO drops.
coupled with various posts here which state to hunt at night- i am betting that the greatest likelihood is that mobs spawned after sunset in the world are VERY likely to have the bile.

i seriously was here about 5 minutes at about 1am game time and got 5 biles.
▲ Page top
Drops
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2010 at 2:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592345320373
10 posts
Got 5 today in around 3 hours, not a single one dropped inside the caves, every one dropped outside.
▲ Page top
omghowlong
QuoteReply
# Sep 22 2006 at 11:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591905037260
2 posts
I killed 25 in a row inside the caves : no drop

Later i killed 75+ in a row (maybe 6) south of the caves (young+elder) : 3 drops

I spent one stack of sow pots. I made about 850pp on all wurms. I killed them all night until late in the morning. I must have been very unlucky but id say u need to be there about 8-10h total for the 5 biles.

Now its more like 125 mobs total, 1k pp made and just got the 4th bile.

I spent 5h on the griffon eggs and tho that was a lot but that was cake compared to this.

I cant trust some of u got 8 biles total or got 5 biles in less then 6h total.

Shardtooth poped 2 times outside and 2 times inside.

Now got all 5 biles FINALLY !!!

So i end up this message, good luck to you tho u will need that.
▲ Page top
omghowlong
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2006 at 5:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591905037260
2 posts
/cry

I failed the final combine with exactly 115 tailoring so I have to farm em again :'(

I heared that someone failed it 6 times so be aware with that freaking part of the quest
▲ Page top
omghowlong
QuoteReply
# Jan 05 2009 at 10:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591791481867
View User Forum
33 posts
By the formulas on various Tradeskilling websites (skill - (.75*trivial) + 51.5 = success percentage), you need a skill of 130 to have a 95 percent chance of success. You can get it down to 124 if you have a Geerlok. This should be fairly easy with beginning Wu's, which you do need the help of an enchanter, but it's mostly enchanters doing this quest (although the shawls can be quite generally useful.

▲ Page top
Rarer then Snow Griffin Eggs
QuoteReply
# May 28 2005 at 1:16 PM Rating: Decent
Narpok
View User Forum
Scholar
342 posts
Well, that's been my experience at least. I spent about 2 and a half hours looking for and killing a dozen griffins to get the eggs. No biggie. But these worms are taking forever to farm! 5 hours in and I only have two bile while following the advice here =\. Crappy luck.

Heads up to solo enchanters (60+). If you wanna knock the worms out easily, charm/dire charm a Frost Giant Elite (immune to cold attacks), drop some clicky-epic-haste on it, and let it go to town.

If you're not one to charm solo, then your level 55 pet will suffice, but the wurms wont drop so quickly. (don't forget to drop both Asphyxiate and Torment of argli on the wurm to help move things along.)

Edited, Sat May 28 14:43:22 2005
▲ Page top
Howto get Glowing Biles
QuoteReply
# May 17 2005 at 10:46 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592790503305
57 posts
Prepared to get 8 Biles today.After reading the posts here i decided to go for young Wurms south of the Wurm Caves(around the big Hut with the new NPC-between Wurm Caves and End of River)Since the 2 Toons i needed em for dont have tracking i just positioned me in the Center of the Area and clicked around me till i got a Wurm on Target pulled em to Center killed looted rinse repeated.Before i got on to young Wurms i tried like 40 Wurms inside the Caves but without a Bile.Outside than i got the 8 Biles in under 1 Hour - all from young Wurms.And its true if you do it by Night with dynamiclight on and Gamma turned down a Bit( take of all your Items with Light Effect or you will have a harder Time to see the Glow) you can see the Glow clearly - its like the Wurms are wearing an Item with Light Effect.But if you ask me ,to go only behind the glowing Wurms is not very clever cuz every Wurm killed can respawn as a Wurm with a Bile so you should just kill all in the Area:)Well good Luck with em Biles:)
▲ Page top
Young Wurms
QuoteReply
# Mar 15 2005 at 4:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592764181945
1 post
I spent 2 hours clearing the caves and tunnels without a single drop. Came back the next day decided to try the young shardwurms south of the cave. Had all 5 within 45 min of starting. Also, there is a very obvious glow on the wurms that have the bile. Atleast, if you have all of your graphics settings on. Mine are turned down and I can't tell a difference, but on my wife's computer the whole top of the wurm glows bright compared to the others.

i've wiped the entire cave except the broodmother and the one strong named. doine it 4 times. only 1 dropped from them sofar tried south of cave and killed young ones and killed abotu 40 of them, no drops off them sofar.

Common Drop: Mob
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2004 at 3:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592761898339
Scholar
73 posts
The elder wurm that patrolls the north mouth of the cave has droped 4/4 biles I have so far, 4/4 times. Have killed at least 100 others and no biles. This one seems to be the best way to get them, will post to confirm wheter or not he drops number 5 on the 5th kill.
▲ Page top
Wow, they do glow!
QuoteReply
# Sep 28 2004 at 5:21 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591768191932
View User Forum
Sage
118 posts
I have killed about 30 Wurms indiscriminately in an attempt to get my 3 glowing worm bile. On my first (north of tunnel) and second (South west of tunnel) success getting a "Glowing Worm Bile" I did not notice any glow. Of course they were both day kills and I probably could not have seen the glow anyway. On my last success I did notice, though it did not strike me until after the kill, that the worm would occasionally illuminate the walls of the cave as it walked toward me although it was not constant. It appeared much like the glow I myself cast on the ice at night as I cross the Great Divide with my Ranger.

So it is true, but anyway you slice it you still have to kill lots of worms to get the "little glow worms" to pop. If you sit around waiting for the glimmering glow worms to slither by then you will NOT have the glow of success beaming from your frost covered face!


P.S. Why is it not wUrm bile instead of wOrm bile?

Edited, Tue Sep 28 06:27:37 2004

▲ Page top
"glowing" wurms
QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2004 at 10:22 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591808186005
Scholar
69 posts
the ones carrying biles do in fact "glow". You guys are probably mis-visualizing "glow".

Wurms with biles light up the ground, but very dim. It's noticeable compared to wurms without bile. The bile is like a lightsource item.
▲ Page top
Drop rate
QuoteReply
# May 09 2004 at 12:39 AM Rating: Decent
SummerC
3 posts
All five biles dropped within three hours for me. One from an Eldar in the cave, four from Youngs South of the cave. Even with gamma turned down could not see any glowing. Slaughter them and they will drop.

D
▲ Page top
Spawns, Cash, and Biles
QuoteReply
# Apr 05 2004 at 2:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782691071
Scholar
35 posts
While I understand and respect the sentiment of those giving instruction on looking for the "glowing wurms" to get the biles, I have found that spending some time (perhaps and hour or two) doing a slaughter fest will yield at least one or two biles.

I focus only on the young wurms in the area south of the wurm caves (approx. pos100, neg6500) as well as any wandering ancients or elder that head south out of the caves.

I part my pet, race to spawn points, have the pet attack and put myself far enough back not to draw aggro. At levels 50+, the pet can easily handle the young wurms while I spend time finding the next victim.

The payoff while waiting for the glowing worm biles is not that bad. Most wurms drop about 3pp per kill, and the ancients will often drop a scaled tunic or a level 44+ spell. No, you won't get rich, but you will have a nice chunk of change.

It is imperative that you keep the killing up in order to spawn the worms with the bile. From my count, I killed 252 wurms in order to get 7 biles (I kept a few to soften the frustration if I fail on a combine).

If you are persistent, the rewards are there.

Good luck to you all.
▲ Page top
RE: Spawns, Cash, and Biles
QuoteReply
# Apr 12 2004 at 7:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966461329
Scholar
19 posts
I've gotten 2 biles so far after tracking and killing every young wurm south of the caves over a 4-hour period. Was beginning to lose hope, but got 2 in a row at the end. Will be going back soon with more patience to get some more.

Killed those 2 during EQ night and had gamma turned down on screen, but saw no evidence of any glowing on, around or under the wurms that dropped the biles. And config for dynamic lighting was on.
▲ Page top
Don't just kill the glowing wurms
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2004 at 12:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249478566
Scholar
84 posts
Have seen several posts that say to hunt down and kill the "GLOWING" wurms. While, I can't really say anything about the validity of the glowing wurm that carries the bile(not confirming or denying it) I will say that if you don't kill the non-bile holding wurms, you'll never spawn the bile carrying wurms. Just kill em all. =)
▲ Page top
Look for the glow
QuoteReply
# Feb 09 2004 at 2:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782492812
4 posts
When hunting for these biles, hunt at night (game time), and look for the shard wurms that glow. If you look at the ground around a shard wurm, you will see an aura of light surrounding a shard wurm that will drop a bile. This was much more reliable for me than trying to tell the difference in whether the "skin" was glowing. You can see the ones with the bile from a mile away. Just watch them walk, and if the light around them moves with them, that's the one you want to kill.

If this post isn't clear, feel free to send me a tell (on Kane Bayle, or EQ Live) if you have any questions.

Draiodoir
Magician - 45th Season
Kane Bayle
▲ Page top
Drops
QuoteReply
# Dec 25 2003 at 3:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259349156
2 posts
Kill young wurms much better drop rate imho.
